question: Does the compact frontier protection body materially improve deterministic task outcomes versus a native-loaded no-op control on current frontier hosts?
estimand: Static compact protection-body effect under explicit host-native skill loading; not the longitudinal compliance-canary hook effect.

loading_contract.frontier: compact body plus arm-specific final marker
loading_contract.off: minimal no-op body plus arm-specific final marker
loading_contract.limitation: OFF contains a minimal loader shim so explicit native activation succeeds symmetrically; it is not a zero-token control.

security.codex: workspace-write sandbox, network disabled, shell environment inherit=none
security.claude: Skill, Read, Edit, Write, and permission-gated Bash(python3 check.py) only; empty strict MCP config

pilot_gates.interpretation: Pilot expands only on a >=10pp paired lift with no scope regression and <=15% median token overhead. A null pilot is a futility signal, not a final retirement verdict.

arm_order: Alternates by case within each lane; sessions and fixtures are never reused.
